title: The Fishing Lesson
introKo:
호숫가에서 받는 아주 건전한 낚시 강습.
…강사님의 지도가 유난히 밀착형이라는 것만 빼면요.
introEn:
A perfectly wholesome fishing lesson by the lake.
...if you ignore how hands-on the instructor is.

quiz.q: 강사 Banks 씨가 제일 먼저 가르친 것은 무엇이었나요?

0000.name: 잔잔한 물가
0000.en: A week later, you wave at Mr. Banks from the far end of the dock, but you keep to your own quiet spot. He nods, casts once, and lets you be. Some fish, you have learned, are best watched from a safe distance.
0000.ko: 일주일 뒤, 당신은 부두 저편에서 Banks 씨에게 손만 흔들어요. 그래도 자기만의 조용한 자리는 지켜요. 그는 고개만 끄덕이고 한 번 던진 뒤 당신을 내버려 둬요. 어떤 물고기는 안전한 거리에서 지켜보는 게 제일 좋다는 걸, 당신은 배웠어요.

0001.name: 느려도 다시 오는 손
0001.en: You were shy with the grip, patient with the wait, and you asked for his hands when the big one pulled. But you did ask to come back, and now you do, every Saturday. "Slow hands," Mr. Banks tells the other anglers, "but he always comes back."
0001.ko: 대를 잡을 땐 수줍었고, 기다림엔 참을성 있었고, 대물이 당길 땐 그의 손을 빌렸죠. 그래도 다시 오겠다는 말만은 했고, 이제 토요일마다 정말 와요. "손은 느려도 꼭 다시 온다니까." Banks 씨가 다른 낚시꾼들에게 말해요.

0010.name: 말없이 큰 걸 끄는 사람
0010.en: You said little all morning, but Mr. Banks remembers one thing: you brought the big one in alone. "Still waters run deep," he tells the whole dock. The heaviest rod now waits just for you.
0010.ko: 아침 내내 말수는 적었지만, Banks 씨는 딱 하나를 기억해요. 대물을 당신 혼자 끌어올렸다는 것. "조용한 물이 깊은 법이지." 그가 부두 사람들 모두에게 말해요. 이제 제일 묵직한 대는 늘 당신을 기다려요.

0011.name: 늦게 무는 손맛
0011.en: You began shy and patient, but by the end you landed the big one alone and asked to come again. Mr. Banks loves that kind of angler. "Quiet at dawn, strong by noon: the best fish bite the same way," he says.
0011.ko: 수줍고 참을성 있게 시작했지만, 끝엔 대물을 혼자 잡고 또 오겠다고까지 했죠. Banks 씨는 그런 낚시꾼을 좋아해요. "동틀 녘엔 조용하다가 정오엔 힘이 넘치는 것 — 제일 좋은 물고기도 그렇게 물어요." 그가 말해요.

0100.name: 성급한 입질
0100.en: It was your first time, and you were careful with everything except your patience. "I like a quick bite," you said, and Mr. Banks still laughs about it. No one on that dock has ever reeled so fast, or waited so little.
0100.ko: 처음이라던 당신은 모든 일에 조심스러웠어요. 참을성만 빼고요. "빨리 입질이 오는 게 좋아요." 그 한마디에 Banks 씨는 아직도 웃어요. 그 부두에서 그렇게 빨리 릴을 감은 사람도, 그렇게 못 기다린 사람도 없었대요.

0101.name: 빠른 손맛의 단골
0101.en: Every weekend you come back, rod already in hand. You are gentle at the start but greedy for the bite, very greedy. "When you fish here," Mr. Banks says, "my lake gets no rest at all."
0101.ko: 주말마다 당신은 이미 대를 손에 쥐고 다시 와요. 시작은 얌전하지만 입질 앞에선 욕심쟁이죠. 아주 욕심쟁이요. "당신이 낚시하는 날엔 우리 호수가 쉴 틈이 없어요." Banks 씨가 말해요.

0110.name: 조용한 손맛꾼
0110.en: You arrived shy, but the water found your gifts: a hungry line and a strong pull, all done alone. You went home without asking for more, and that is the part Mr. Banks cannot understand. "All that talent," he sighs at the dock, "and he came only once."
0110.ko: 수줍게 왔지만, 물이 당신의 재능을 찾아냈죠. 굶주린 줄과 강한 당김을, 전부 혼자서요. 그런데 더 오겠단 말도 없이 집에 갔고, Banks 씨는 그게 이해가 안 된대요. "그 재능을 가지고 딱 한 번만 오다니." 그가 부두에서 한숨을 쉬어요.

0111.name: 늦게 트인 손
0111.en: You said it was your first time, and maybe it was. But by noon you were reeling like a veteran, landing the big one alone, and booking next weekend too. "Anglers like you," Mr. Banks says, "are why I still love teaching."
0111.ko: 처음이라고 했죠. 정말 그랬을지도 몰라요. 그런데 정오쯤엔 베테랑처럼 릴을 감고, 대물도 혼자 잡고, 다음 주말까지 예약했어요. "당신 같은 낚시꾼 때문에 아직도 가르치는 게 좋다니까요." Banks 씨가 말해요.

1000.name: 큰소리 새내기
1000.en: "I'm good with my hands," you said, then you asked about every little step and let him guide the rest. Mr. Banks found it charming. "Big talk, careful hands: honestly, the safest kind of student," he says.
1000.ko: "저 손재주가 좀 있어요"라더니, 그 뒤로는 한 단계마다 물어보고 나머지는 그에게 맡겼죠. Banks 씨는 그게 오히려 귀엽대요. "말은 크고 손은 조심스럽고 — 사실 그런 학생이 제일 안전해요." 그가 말해요.

1001.name: 약속을 지키는 남자
1001.en: You gripped with confidence, fished with care, and kept your promise to return. Now every Saturday has the same plan: his lake, your hands. "Some men only talk," Mr. Banks tells his friends. "Mine shows up."
1001.ko: 자신 있게 대를 잡고, 조심스럽게 낚시하고, 다시 오겠다는 약속은 지켰죠. 이제 토요일마다 계획은 똑같아요. 그의 호수, 당신의 손. "말만 하는 남자도 많은데, 우리 학생은 진짜로 와요." Banks 씨가 친구들에게 말해요.

1010.name: 부두의 해결사
1010.en: Good with your hands and strong enough to land the big one alone: that is how Mr. Banks describes you now. You never asked to come back, but somehow the biggest fish always bite when you walk onto his dock. Funny how that works.
1010.ko: 손재주 좋고, 대물도 혼자 끌어올리는 사람. 요즘 Banks 씨가 당신을 그렇게 소개해요. 다시 오겠단 말은 안 했는데, 이상하게 제일 큰 물고기들은 꼭 당신이 부두에 올라설 때 입질을 해요. 참 신기한 일이죠.

1011.name: 떠오르는 손맛왕
1011.en: Confident grip, patient hands, the big one landed alone, and you booked next weekend before he could even ask. The other anglers keep asking who his new star is. Mr. Banks just smiles and says nothing at all.
1011.ko: 자신 있는 손, 참을성 있는 손길, 혼자 끌어올린 대물. 게다가 그가 묻기도 전에 다음 주말을 예약했죠. 다른 낚시꾼들은 그의 새 에이스가 누구냐고 자꾸 물어요. Banks 씨는 그저 웃으며 아무 말도 안 해요.

1100.name: 요란한 입질
1100.en: You promised strong hands and chased every bite like a storm, but you let him take the big one and left with a polite goodbye. Mr. Banks still tells the story: "He came, he cast, he went home early." Then he looks at his quiet lake and smiles.
1100.ko: 힘 좋은 손을 약속했고, 입질마다 소나기처럼 달려들었죠. 그래도 대물은 그에게 맡기고, 인사만 남긴 채 일찍 갔어요. Banks 씨는 아직도 그 얘길 해요. "왔고, 던졌고, 일찍 집에 갔죠." 그러고는 조용해진 호수를 보며 웃어요.

1101.name: 목마른 단골
1101.en: Confident from the first hello, greedy for every bite, and booked for every weekend after. The only thing you did not do alone was the big one: you asked for his hands, and he loved that most of all. "The best fishing," he says, "is done by four hands."
1101.ko: 첫인사부터 자신만만, 입질마다 욕심, 주말은 전부 예약. 혼자 하지 않은 건 대물 하나뿐이었죠. 그의 손을 빌렸고, 그는 그게 제일 좋았대요. "낚시는 손이 넷일 때 제일 잘 되는 법이에요." 그가 말해요.

1110.name: 좋은 꿈 같은 사람
1110.en: Strong grip, hungry line, the big one landed alone: you did it all, called his lake amazing, and went home. Mr. Banks stood on the dock a long time. Some students, he thinks, are like a good dream: wonderful, and gone too soon.
1110.ko: 강한 손, 굶주린 줄, 혼자 끌어올린 대물까지. 전부 해내고는 호수가 멋지다는 말만 남기고 집에 갔죠. Banks 씨는 한참 부두에 서 있었어요. 어떤 학생은 좋은 꿈 같아요. 근사한데, 너무 빨리 사라지죠.

1111.name: 소문난 손맛의 주인
1111.en: By summer, the whole town talks about Mr. Banks and his lake, which has never given up so many fish. He tells his friends you have the surest hands on the water. And the anglers notice one small thing: his boat is never tied up on weekends anymore.
1111.ko: 여름이 되자 온 마을이 Banks 씨와 그 호수 이야기를 해요. 호수가 이렇게 많은 고기를 내준 적이 없었거든요. 그는 친구들에게 당신이 물에서 손이 제일 야무지다고 자랑해요. 그리고 낚시꾼들은 작은 변화 하나를 눈치채요. 주말이면 그의 배가 이제 묶여 있는 법이 없다는 걸요.

normal.text: Saturday morning at the lake. The water is calm and quiet. Your fishing instructor, Mr. Banks, is waiting for you on the dock with a warm smile.
"Welcome," he says, handing you a long rod. "For the next two hours, you are all mine. Just you, me, and the fish. Are you ready?"
"First, the grip. Hold the rod firmly, and don't be shy with it. A loose hand loses the big ones. Hold it like you mean it."

options.en: Don't worry. I'm good with my hands.
options.ko: 걱정 마세요. 저 손재주가 좀 있어요.
options.reaction: "I can tell," he says. "That is a firm grip."

options.en: Like this? It is my first time holding one.
options.ko: 이렇게요? 이런 거 잡아 보는 건 처음이라서요.
options.reaction: "Everyone's first time is," he smiles. "I will guide your hands."

normal.text: "Now, the cast." He shows you the wrist snap. "It is all in the wrist. One soft flick, and the line flies far. Go on, try it."
The line lands with a small splash. "Good. Now let it hang, and wait. Out here, patience is everything. The best catch never comes fast."

options.en: How long do we usually wait?
options.reaction: "As long as it takes," he says. "The good ones always make you wait."

options.en: I am not a patient man. I like a quick bite.
options.ko: 저 참을성이 별로 없어요. 빨리 입질이 오는 게 좋아요.
options.reaction: "Is that so?" He laughs. "Then you and I will get along just fine."

normal.text: The tip of the rod dips. "There," Mr. Banks whispers, leaning close. "A bite. Feel that? Nice and steady. Don't rush it."
The rod bends hard. "Oho, a big one today," he says, his eyes bright. "Size is not everything out here, but it never hurts."
"Now reel it in, slowly. Feel the tension. When I say so, pull harder." Your heart is pounding.

options.en: I have got it. Watch me bring it in alone.
options.ko: 제가 잡았어요. 혼자 끌어올리는 거 보세요.
options.reaction: "Look at you," he says. "Confidence suits you."

options.en: Can you hold the rod with me? It is stronger than I thought.
options.ko: 대를 같이 잡아 주실래요? 생각보다 힘이 세요.
options.reaction: "Of course. Four hands land the big ones."

normal.text: Together, or alone, you land it at last. A fat, shining fish flips on the dock. "What a catch," Mr. Banks says, clearly pleased.

options.en: This was perfect. Your lake is amazing, Mr. Banks.
options.ko: 완벽했어요. 여기 호수 정말 대단하네요, Banks 씨.
options.reaction: "You have got the touch. My last student never had this much patience."

options.en: I want to do this again. Can I come back next weekend?
options.ko: 또 하고 싶어요. 다음 주말에 다시 와도 돼요?
options.reaction: "You should. Fishing is like riding a bicycle. The body never forgets."

normal.text: The sun climbs high over the water as you head home, arms tired and bucket full. Fishing, you decide, is the finest way to spend a Saturday.

hard.text: Saturday morning settles over the lake, calm and silver, and no one welcomes it more than Mr. Banks, whose lakeside lessons are quietly famous around town. He is waiting for you on the dock with a warm, knowing smile.
"Welcome," he says, pressing a long rod into your hands like something precious. "For the next two hours you are all mine, just you, me, and whatever is hungry down there. Shall we begin?"
"First, the grip. Hold the rod firmly and don't be shy about it, because a loose hand loses every big one. Hold it like you actually mean it."

options.en: Don't worry. I have always been good with my hands.
options.ko: 걱정 마세요. 저 원래 손재주가 좋아요.
options.reaction: "I can tell," he says, watching your fingers. "That is a firm grip indeed."

options.en: Like this? It is honestly my first time holding one.
options.ko: 이렇게요? 솔직히 이런 거 잡아 보는 건 처음이라서요.
options.reaction: "Everyone's first time is," he smiles. "Don't worry, I will guide your hands through it."

hard.text: "Now for the cast." He demonstrates the wrist snap, slow and deliberate. "It is all in the wrist. One clean flick and the line sails out. Go on, try it yourself."
Your line lands with a soft splash. "Good. Now let it hang loose and wait. Out here, patience is the whole game, and the best catch is the one that makes you wait for it."

options.en: How long do we usually have to wait?
options.reaction: "As long as it takes," he says calmly. "The good ones always make you wait the longest."

options.en: I will be honest. I am not a patient man, and I like a quick bite.
options.ko: 솔직히 말할게요. 저 참을성이 별로 없어서, 빨리 입질이 오는 게 좋아요.
options.reaction: "Is that so?" He laughs softly. "Then you and I are going to get along just fine."

hard.text: The tip of the rod dips sharply. "There it is," Mr. Banks whispers, leaning in close beside you. "A bite. Feel that pull? Nice and steady, and whatever you do, don't rush it."
The rod bends into a deep curve. "Oho, a big one today," he murmurs, his eyes lighting up. "Size is not everything out here, of course, though it certainly never hurts."
"Now reel it in, nice and slow. Feel that tension building? Steady now, and the moment I tell you, pull harder." Your heart is pounding against your ribs.

options.en: I have got it. Just watch me bring this one in alone.
options.ko: 제가 잡았어요. 이건 혼자 끌어올리는 거 잘 보세요.
options.reaction: "Well, look at you," he murmurs. "Confidence certainly suits you."

options.en: Could you hold the rod with me? It is stronger than I expected.
options.ko: 대를 같이 잡아 주실래요? 생각보다 힘이 세네요.
options.reaction: "Of course, of course. Four hands land the big ones."

hard.text: Together, or entirely alone, you land it at last, and a fat, glistening fish flips across the wooden dock. "Now that is a catch," Mr. Banks says, clearly and thoroughly pleased.

options.en: That was perfect. Your lake is truly amazing, Mr. Banks.
options.ko: 완벽했어요. 여기 호수 정말 대단하네요, Banks 씨.
options.reaction: "You have got a real touch. My last student never had half this patience."

options.en: I want to do this again. May I come back next weekend?
options.ko: 또 하고 싶어요. 다음 주말에 다시 와도 될까요?
options.reaction: "You certainly should. Fishing is like riding a bicycle, and the body never forgets."

hard.text: The sun climbs high over the water as you head home, your arms pleasantly tired and your bucket full. Fishing, you decide, is the finest way a man can spend a Saturday.

across.en: from one side to the other

along.en: get along means to be friendly

arrived.en: came to a place (past of arrive)

ask.en: to say a question to someone

beside.en: right next to someone or something

bite.en: when a fish pulls on the line; a small pull with the mouth

boat.en: a small vehicle that floats on water

bucket.en: a round container with a handle

called.en: said it was; named (past of call)

cast.en: throwing the fishing line out; to throw it

clearly.en: in a way easy to see

confidence.en: the feeling that you can do it

dawn.en: the time when the sun first rises

do.en: to make or perform an action

dock.en: a wooden path built out over the water

dream.en: what you see in your mind while sleeping

even.en: used to show something is surprising

expected.en: thought it would happen (past of expect)

eyes.en: the parts of your face you see with

fat.en: big and round with lots of flesh

feel.en: to sense something in your body

fingers.en: the parts at the end of your hand

fish.en: an animal that lives in water; to try to catch fish

fished.en: tried to catch fish (past of fish)

found.en: discovered; thought it was (past of find)

friends.en: people you know and like well

game.ko: 게임, 승부, 관건 (the whole game: 전부)
game.en: the whole point; what matters most

get.en: to reach a state; get along means to be friendly

goodbye.en: a word you say when you leave

got.ko: 잡았다, 가졌다 (get의 과거형; have got: 가지고 있다)
got.en: held; have got means to have

greedy.en: wanting much more than you need

grip.en: the way you hold something in your hand

guide.en: to lead or show the way

hang.en: to let something hang down loosely

hello.en: a word you say when you meet someone

high.en: at or to a great height

instructor.en: a person who teaches a skill

kept.en: did not break; held (past of keep)

lake.en: a large area of water with land all around

land.en: to bring a fish out of the water

landed.en: brought a fish out of the water (past of land)

landing.en: bringing a fish out of the water

lighting.en: light up means to become bright

line.en: the thin string on a fishing rod

may.en: a polite word to ask permission

mean.en: to be serious about; to have a meaning

most.ko: 가장, 제일 (most of all: 그 무엇보다)

mr.en: a title before a man's name

nods.en: moves the head down to say yes

noon.en: twelve o'clock in the middle of the day

notice.en: to see and become aware of

oho.en: a sound showing surprise or interest

onto.en: to a position on top of

or.en: a word for choosing between things

own.en: belonging to you and no one else

plan.en: an idea of what to do

promise.en: words you say you will keep

promised.en: said you would surely do it (past of promise)

pulled.en: moved something toward you (past of pull)

reel.ko: 릴을 감다; 릴 (reel in: 감아 들이다)
reel.en: to wind the line in; the round part that holds the line

reeled.en: wound the line in (past of reel)

rest.en: the part that is left; a break

ribs.en: the curved bones around your chest

rod.en: the long stick used for fishing

saturday.en: the first day of the weekend

shall.en: a polite word to suggest doing something

silver.en: the shiny grey color of silver

something.en: a thing you do not name

splash.en: the sound of something hitting water

star.en: the best or most admired person

stood.en: was on his feet (past of stand)

summer.en: the hot season of the year

sun.en: the bright star that gives us light

talent.en: something you are naturally good at

teaching.en: the work of helping people learn

tell.en: to say; to know by looking

tension.en: how tight or stretched something is

tied.ko: 묶인, 묶었다 (tie의 과거분사; tie up: 매어 두다)

touch.ko: 손감각, 솜씨 (a good touch: 좋은 솜씨)

waters.ko: 물 (격언 still waters run deep의 waters)
waters.en: water, as in the old saying

wave.en: to move your hand to say hello

we.en: you and I; a group with me

welcome.en: a friendly word said to a guest

which.en: a word that joins extra information

wrist.en: the joint between your hand and arm

wink.firmly: '대를 꽉, 부끄러워 말고 잡으라'는 말은 낚시 교본에 정말 나오는 조언이에요. 다만 그 목소리 톤까지 교본에 적혀 있진 않죠.
wink.bite: '입질이 좋다'는 건 물고기 얘기예요. Banks 씨는 분명 물고기 얘기라고 할 거예요.
wink.big: '대물이네요, 크기가 전부는 아니지만 나쁠 건 없죠' — 낚시터에서 나올 법한 말이에요. 낚시터에서만요.
wink.tension: '줄의 팽팽함을 느껴 보라'는 건 챔질의 기본이에요. 왜 하필 그 순간 심장이 뛰는지는 행간에 있고요.
wink.harder: '이제 더 세게 당겨요' — 릴링의 정석이에요. 정석인데 왜 이렇게 들리는지는 각자의 몫이죠.
wink.wrist: '전부 손목에 달렸다'는 캐스팅 조언이에요. 손목 스냅 얘기가 이렇게 은근할 수 있다는 걸 낚시가 알려주네요.
wink.four: 손이 넷이면 대물을 잡는다는 말 — 낚시에선 협동, 행간에선 밀착이에요. 어느 쪽인지는 강사만 알죠.
wink.patient: '참을성 없는 남자, 빠른 입질이 좋다'는 낚시 취향 고백이에요. 낚시 취향이요.

winkEn.firmly: "Hold it firmly, don't be shy" is genuine advice from any fishing manual. The manual just never specifies the tone of voice.
winkEn.bite: "A good bite" is a fish thing. Mr. Banks will absolutely insist it is a fish thing.
winkEn.big: "A big one, size is not everything but it never hurts" is the sort of line you hear at a lake. Only at a lake.
winkEn.tension: "Feel the tension" is basic reel work. Why your heart chooses that exact moment to race lives between the lines.
winkEn.harder: "Now pull harder" is textbook reeling. It is textbook. Why it lands the way it does is your own business.
winkEn.wrist: "It is all in the wrist" is casting advice. Fishing shows you how quietly a wrist-snap joke can be made.
winkEn.four: "Four hands land the big ones": in fishing, teamwork; between the lines, closeness. Only the instructor knows which he means.
winkEn.patient: "Not a patient man, likes a quick bite" is a confession of fishing taste. Fishing taste.
